content.seo_title: Build a Portfolio: Email Automation for Students
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' trap by building production-grade email automation. Master Antigravity and Claude Code in this intermediate guide for AI builders.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' barrier is a myth shattered by real-world utility. By building an automated email engine, you demonstrate mastery over API orchestration, event-driven architecture, and data flow. This project transforms you from a student into a builder, providing a tangible, high-value asset that proves your technical competence to hiring managers.
content.intro:
You stare at the blank screen, the weight of a dozen tutorials behind you, yet the 'Portfolio' tab remains empty. Every job description demands experience you haven't been given, and every project feels like a toy. The industry doesn't care about your coursework; they care about your ability to solve messy, real-world problems that keep businesses running.

We are going to bridge that gap. Today, we build a robust, autonomous email distribution system. This isn't just a script; it is a scalable backend service that proves you understand the intersection of AI, automation, and reliability. By the end of this guide, you won't just have code on GitHub—you'll have a system that creates value.

steps.title: Define the Architecture
steps.description: Establish the event-driven workflow using Antigravity to define the data schema and trigger mechanisms for your automated email system.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an architectural schema for an email automation service that processes user data, handles template rendering, and integrates with SendGrid/Resend APIs.

steps.title: Implement Robust Error Handling
steps.description: Build a resilient system that handles API rate limits and failed delivery attempts gracefully.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust retry logic middleware for email dispatching, including exponential backoff and logging for failed requests.

steps.title: AI-Driven Content Generation
steps.description: Integrate Claude to dynamically personalize emails based on user segments, moving beyond static templates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate a prompt-chaining service that uses Claude to generate personalized email body content based on user-provided profile data.

steps.title: Data Persistence and Queueing
steps.description: Ensure your automation doesn't lose data by implementing a queue system to manage high-volume requests.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a queueing system using Redis or an equivalent to persist pending email tasks and ensure atomic processing.

steps.title: Deployment and Monitoring
steps.description: Deploy your service and set up telemetry so you can prove the system's uptime and performance in your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure observability endpoints and health checks to monitor email delivery rates and latency.

pro_tips: Always wrap your third-party API calls in an abstraction layer to swap providers without touching core business logic.
Implement idempotency keys for every email sent to ensure that network blips don't result in duplicate customer messages.
Store your email templates in a version-controlled repository or database, never hardcode them into your logic.
Use structured logging with unique correlation IDs to trace an email's lifecycle from trigger to delivery across distributed services.
Add a 'dry-run' mode to your CLI tool to test email generation without actually hitting the SMTP provider during development.
content.next_steps: Once the system is live, document the architecture in a README.md, record a 60-second video demoing the email flow, and pin it to the top of your GitHub profile.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer a student; you are an engineer with a functional, production-grade system in your portfolio. The market rewards builders who solve real problems, not those who hoard certificates. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
